# Malformed v1 dashboard corpus

Hand-testing fixtures for the v1→v2 (Perses) migration. Each file is an importable
v1 dashboard that isolates **one** catalogued malformation (see
`../../LEGACY_DASHBOARD_HANDLING.md`). Workflow:

1. Import a file into the **v1 UI** and note how it renders.
2. Migrate it and open the **v2 UI**; compare.

## Why `version: "v5"`

The query/widget fixtures are stamped `version: "v5"` on purpose. That makes the
v4→v5 SQL migrator (`pkg/transition`) **skip** them (it short-circuits on the
version tag), so the *pre-v5 bodies* reach the Perses converter un-normalized —
exactly the "mislabeled-v5" case the converter's `normalizePreV5*` handlers exist
for. A `version: "v4"` fixture would be fixed by the migrator first and never
exercise those handlers.

## Migration outcome

Outcomes are asserted by `TestMalformedV1FixturesMigrate` (run
`go test ./pkg/types/dashboardtypes/ -run FixturesMigrate -v`). `ok` means
*migrates and validates without error* — **not** *renders correct data*; that is
what the v2 UI check is for.

| # | File | Malformation (catalog #) | v1 UI — expected | Migration |
|---|------|--------------------------|------------------|-----------|
| 01 | `01_having_array` | `having` as v4 array `[{columnName,op,value}]` (1) | normalizes, renders | **ok** — `normalizePreV5Having` |
| 02 | `02_filters_items_deprecated_ops` | `filters{items}` + deprecated ops `nin`/`regex`/`nlike` (2,6) | normalizes, renders | **ok** — `normalizePreV5Filters` |
| 03 | `03_logs_agg_expression_messy` | logs agg exprs: inline `as`, multi-part, trailing junk, empty (3) | parses, renders | **ok** — `normalizePreV5LogTraceAggregations` |
| 04 | `04_flat_metric_aggregation` | metric flat v4 fields, no `aggregations[]` (9) | builds agg, renders | **ok** — `normalizePreV5MetricAggregations` |
| 05 | `05_flat_logs_aggregation` | **logs** flat v4 fields, no `aggregations[]` (9) | builds `count()`, renders | **ok** — `normalizePreV5LogTraceAggregations` builds the expression from flat fields |
| 06 | `06_pagesize_to_limit` | legacy `pageSize` instead of `limit` (table) (8) | uses `limit \|\| pageSize` | **ok** — `normalizePreV5PageSize` |
| 07 | `07_selectcolumns_v5shape_and_empty` | `selectColumns` in v5 `{name}` shape + an empty column (5) | `name ?? key`, drops empty | **ok** — `normalizePreV5SelectColumns` |
| 08 | `08_old_field_keys_list_panel` | list panel `selectedLogFields` in old `{key,dataType,type}` (4,19) | `key→name`, renders | **ok** — `normalizePreV5FieldKeys` |
| 09 | `09_variables_textbox_order_selectedvalue` | `textboxValue`, missing `order`/`id`, `selectedValue` string\|array (10,11,13) | backfills, renders selectors | **ok** — 3 variables |
| 10 | `10_variable_dynamic_missing_attribute` | DYNAMIC var missing `dynamicVariablesAttribute` | dead/empty variable | **readback-fail** — whole dashboard rejected (`DynamicVariableSpec.Name` required); the nvidia-dcgm case |
| 11 | `11_widget_display_fields` | `spanGaps` number/bool, `decimalPrecision` string, `timePreferance` (15–18) | coerces, renders | **ok** — 4 panels |
| 12 | `12_layout_duplicate_i` | same widget id twice in `layout`, different geometry (L2) | dedupes, keeps first | **ok** — deduped, 1 grid item for the widget |
| 13 | `13_layout_orphan_entries` | `layout` entries for a deleted widget + `__dropping-elem__` (L1) | ignores orphans | **ok** — orphans dropped |
| 14 | `14_layout_collapsed_ghost_child_bare_panelmap` | collapsed section with a ghost child + bare-array `panelMap` (L5,L6) | renders section; bare-array = not collapsed | **ok** — ghost dropped, bare-array tolerated (2 grids) |
| 15 | `15_layout_unsorted_nonzero_coords` | unsorted / non-zero-based `x`/`y` (L3,L4) | sorts, renders | **ok** — sorted, `y` rebased to 0 |
| 16 | `16_unrenderable_widget_type` | a widget with an unknown/`EMPTY_WIDGET` panel type + layout entry | shows an empty placeholder card | **convert-fail** — the unknown type is noted, so the *whole* dashboard is rejected (it never reaches the layout dangling-ref handling) |

## Regenerating

Fixtures are generated by `gen_fixtures.py` in this directory — run it from the
repo root (`python3 pkg/types/dashboardtypes/testdata/malformed_v1/gen_fixtures.py`)
to regenerate or add cases. Every fixture must have an entry in
`expectedFixtureOutcomes` (in `perses_v1_to_v2_fixtures_test.go`) or the test fails.
